text: We present to you a video on robotic retroperitoneal adenlectomy for large adrenal lesion, its feasibility and outcome. Robotic adenlectomy is becoming the treatment option of choice for large adrenal tumors. However, there is a risk of capsular disruption, incomplete removal and the associated technical difficulties. The retroperitoneal route provides a direct approach with no bowel handling. We present to you the perioperative outcomes of robotic adrenalectomy over a period of three years and we present a video of such a case.

The patient is positioned in the left lateral position at the edge of the table. The initial entry point is just below the twelfth rib near the mid-axillary line, using a visiport for direct entry into the retroperitoneal space. A zero-degree camera is used in a sweeping motion to create space. The first robotic port is inserted, followed by the creation of additional space with bipolar forceps for the insertion of two more robotic ports. A 12 mm assistant port is placed between the first two ports. The final setup shows four robotic ports and one assistant port. The DaVinci XI robot is docked, and dissection is carried out cranially along the psoas muscle.

The surgeon identifies the adrenal tumor and performs dissection around it, initially separating it from the underlying psoas muscle and fascia. Bipolar cautery is used to ligate small feeding vessels supplying the tumor. A dissection plane is created between the upper pole of the kidney and the tumor, and the upper part of the tumor is separated from the peritoneal lining over the liver. A portion of the tumor is dissected from beneath the inferior vena cava (IVC), utilizing the retroperitoneal approach for safe access to feeder vessels behind the IVC.

The surgeon performs further dissection to identify the main adrenal vein, which is clipped with wax clips and then cut. Following this, the remaining attachments of the adrenal gland are released and cut using monopolar cautery. The specimen is then placed into an endo bag for removal.
The video shows the floor of the dissection where part of the adrenal gland extends behind the inferior vena cava (IVC). The resected right adrenal specimen is displayed.
